topic.name: Government Appropriations and Authorizations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts related to the appropriation of funds and authorization of programs across multiple sectors including defense, intelligence, and conservation.

Document 1: Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to authorize additional FY2009-FY2011 appropriations for the family caregiver support program.
Authorizes additional FY2009-FY2011 appropriations for the National Clearinghouse for Long-Term Care Information.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to Congress on the advisability of providing federal retirement benefits to U.S. citizens for their service prior to 1977.
operated by the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA).
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Air Force to acquire real property and carry out military construction projects at Cannon Air Force Base, New Mexico.
Authorizes appropriations for FY2008 for such purposes.
Document 4: Amends the Asian Elephant Conservation Act of 1997 to authorize appropriations for FY2007-FY2012 for such Act.
Increase the amount that may be used for administrative expenses to carry out such Act.
Document 5: Authorizes FY2016 appropriations for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities.
Specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings as of September 30, 2016, for such activities are those in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 6: Appropriates funds for FY2011 for the Department of Defense (DOD) for military construction.
Specifies restrictions and authorizations regarding the use of funds appropriated in this title.
Document 7: Increases the maximum amount of funds that may be obligated for carrying out actual construction to modify an existing dam.
Requires the Secretary to submit to such Committees written notice that provides a summary of the project.
Document 8: Authorizes appropriations for FY2013 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities.
Specifies that the amounts authorized above and the authorized personnel ceilings for such activities are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations.
Document 9: Makes FY 2008 appropriations for the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers for civil functions.
Sets forth limitations upon reprogramming of funds and certain continuing contracts.
Document 10: Authorizes appropriations for FY2012 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities.
Requires notification of the congressional intelligence committees in either case.

topic.name: Television and Communications Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts and amendments related to the regulation of television broadcasting, communication services, and digital media in the United States.

Document 1: Repeals provisions of the Communications Act of 1934 concerning the carriage of distant television stations and significantly viewed signals by satellite carriers.
Extends exemptions from copyright infringement laws to certain secondary transmissions by cable systems and satellite carriers.
Document 2: Amends the Digital Television Transition and Public Safety Act of 2005 to extend the requirement for payments from the Digital Television Transition and Public Safety Fund.
Allows the Assistant Secretary to extend, on a case-by-case basis, the period of performance for any investment approved under the program.
Document 3: Amends federal copyright law and the Communications Act of 1934 to allow satellite or cable retransmission of signals of broadcast television stations located in the capital of a state to counties in that state if the counties meet specified criteria.
Document 4: Amends the Internet Tax Freedom Act to make permanent the ban on state and local taxation of Internet access and on multiple or discriminatory taxes on electronic commerce.
Document 5: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to allow the renewal of a full-power analog television broadcasting license through February 17, 2013.
Prohibits the FCC from extending or renewing a full-power television broadcast license that authorizes analog service on or after February 17, 2013.
Document 6: Makes it unlawful for a person during specified periods before a federal primary or general election to make certain prerecorded political telephone calls without disclosing immediately the recorded nature of the calls.
Document 7: Repeals provisions of the Communications Act of 1934 concerning the carriage of distant television stations and significantly viewed signals by satellite carriers.
Directs the FCC to repeal related FCC rules and eliminate restrictions on the number of broadcast television stations.
Document 8: Amends the Twenty-first Century Communications and Video Accessibility Act of 2010 to make certain technical corrections.
Requires the Video Programming and Emergency Access Advisory Committee to identify additional protocols for the delivery of emergency information.
Document 9: Amends the Communications Act of 1934 to prohibit the FCC from requiring broadcasters to present opposing viewpoints on controversial issues of public importance.
Commonly referred to as the Fairness Doctrine.
Document 10: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to repeal the excise tax on communication services.
Repeals the excise tax on local telephone service, toll telephone service, and teletypewriter exchange service.

topic.name: Federal Election Campaign Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative amendments aimed at reforming the Federal Election Campaign Act to regulate campaign expenditures, contributions, and voting processes.

Document 1: Limits expenditures by candidates for the House of Representatives to $500,000 during the election cycle.
Sets maximum amounts for primary and general elections.
Document 2: Applies the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals to domestic corporations with foreign shareholders.
Document 3: Repeals the limitation on party expenditures on behalf of candidates in general elections.
Document 5: Requires the presidential designee for the Federal Voting Assistance Program to have experience in election administration.
Document 6: Sets forth specified expenditure limitations for House of Representatives general elections.
Establishes the Grassroots Good Citizenship Fund for public funding of House candidates.
Document 7: Revises the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the FEC.
Document 8: Requires all candidates for the House of Representatives to run in a single open primary.
Expresses the sense of Congress regarding a day off for employees on general election day.
Document 9: Eliminates limitations on direct contributions from individuals and political committees to candidates in federal elections.
Document 10: Provides that graduated income tax rates applicable to principal campaign committees of candidates for Congress shall apply to state and local offices.

topic.name: Education Policy and Reform
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at improving educational outcomes, accountability, and support for diverse student populations in K-12 education.

Document 1: require annual state report cards to disaggregate, by type of disability data regarding the educational outcomes of students with disabilities.
Document 2: revise the program allotting grants to states to develop and administer state academic content and achievement standards and assessments of student progress toward those standards.
Document 3: establish a principal recruitment and training grant program awarding renewable, matching grants to states, local educational agencies (LEAs), educational service agencies, nonprofit organizations, institutions of higher education.
Document 4: award grants to state educational agencies (SEAs) in order to award subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAs) to assist public schools with the increased costs of fuel for school buses and energy for public school buildings.
Document 5: revise the Small, Rural School Achievement program, which gives rural local educational agencies (LEAs) federal formula grants and greater flexibility in the use of state educational funds.
Document 6: allot matching grants to states and, through them, subgrants to local educational agencies (LEAs), childhood education program providers, or consortia of those entities to implement high-quality prekindergarten programs.
Document 7: provide that in all competitive grants awarded by the Department of Education to states or local educational agencies (LEAs) or by states to LEAs under the Act, 10% of the competitive preference points may be awarded to states and LEAs that propose to use research-proven programs.
Document 8: establish a grants program to reduce class size through the use of fully-qualified teachers.
Document 9: direct the Secretary of Education to establish a Grow Your Own Teacher program making competitive grants to eligible entities to train and employ certain individuals in hard-to-staff schools.
Document 10: alter the frequency with which students must take the tests used in determining whether they are making adequate yearly progress (AYP) toward state academic achievement standards.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation and Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the conservation, protection, and management of wildlife and natural resources in various designated areas.

Document 1: Huna Tlingit Traditional Gull Egg Use Act of 2011 authorizes egg collection for traditional purposes.
Requires annual planning with the Hoonah Indian Association.
Document 2: Scotchman Peaks Wilderness Act designates wilderness area for conservation.
Ensures access for Indian tribes for traditional purposes.
Document 3: Fish Hatchery Protection Act prohibits significant changes to fish hatchery programs.
Protects aquatic species propagation within the National Fish Hatchery System.
Document 8: Amends Great Sand Dunes National Park to focus on habitat restoration for native species.
Emphasizes migratory bird conservation in management.
Document 9: Lesser Prairie Chicken Voluntary Recovery Act monitors conservation efforts for the species.
Prohibits treatment as threatened without meeting conservation goals.
Document 10: Establishes Berryessa Snow Mountain National Conservation Area for ecological and wildlife protection.
Requires management plans to address climate change and conservation issues.

topic.name: Financial Regulation Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments and reforms related to financial regulation and oversight in the U.S. financial system.

Document 1: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to repeal the requirement for regulations requiring each covered financial institution to disclose the structures of all incentive-based compensation arrangements it offers.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act to prohibit an insured credit union from making any member business loan that would result in the total amount of such loans outstanding at that credit union at any one time exceeding either: (1) 1.75 times the actual net worth of the credit union, or (2) 12.25% of the total assets of the credit union.
Document 3: Amends the Inspector General Act of 1978 to repeal the authority of the Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System (Board) to appoint the Inspector General of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB).
Document 4: Amends federal bankruptcy law with respect to a 'covered financial corporation' incorporated or organized under any federal or state law.
Document 5: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act concerning establishment of minimum leverage and minimum risk-based capital requirements.
Document 6: Establishes an independent Financial Stability Council to review, and approve or prohibit issuance of, or require modification of, any rule or regulation issued by any federal financial regulator.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to prohibit an insured depository institution from being or becoming an affiliate of any insurance company, securities entity, or swaps entity.
Document 8: Abolishes the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and each federal reserve bank.
Document 9: Amends federal law to authorize interest-bearing or dividend-bearing transaction accounts for all businesses.
Document 10: Establishes the bipartisan Commission on Catastrophic Disaster Risk and Insurance to assess the condition of the property and casualty insurance and reinsurance markets.

topic.name: Homeland Security and Privacy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the Department of Homeland Security's authority, privacy standards, and security measures in various contexts.

Document 1: National Guard to provide assistance to support civilian authority firefighting operations
aerial firefighting employment of the Modular Airborne Firefighting System.
Document 3: sets forth standards and procedures for Department of Homeland Security (DHS) border searches
prohibits a DHS official from considering race, ethnicity, national origin, or religion
Document 4: directs the Secretary of Homeland Security to designate a full-time privacy official
monitoring the component's compliance with all applicable federal privacy laws
Document 5: directs airport perimeter screening of all individuals, goods, property
authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security to hire necessary screeners
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security to provide additional U.S. Border Patrol agents
combat drug trafficking and smuggling of firearms
Document 8: places the Transportation Security Administration under the administration of the Department of Homeland Security
develop partnerships with state and local law enforcement agencies to improve coordination

revise the definition of 'adulterated'

treat a serious life-threatening disease

from commencing to manufacture, market

ensure that such amended procedures give priority

meet certain ASTM International standards
Document 8: require foreign manufacturers to establish a registered agent

topic.name: Animal Welfare and Protection Legislation
topic.description: The documents collectively address legislative measures aimed at the welfare and protection of animals, particularly in commercial breeding and military service contexts.

Document 1: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder'
Requires dealers to include on licensing applications and annual renewals the total number of dogs exempted from exercise
Document 4: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to define a 'high volume retail breeder'
Requires the Secretary of Agriculture to promulgate requirements for the exercise of dogs at facilities owned or operated by a dealer
Document 7: Amends the Animal Welfare Act to revise the list of permissible sources of dogs and cats used by research facilities
Monetary penalties are established for violations
Document 10: Directs the Secretary of Defense to classify military working dogs as canine members of the Armed Forces

topic.name: Small Business Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at supporting and regulating small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: Subcontracting Transparency and Reliability Act of 2012 - Amends the Small Business Act
prohibit a small business receiving a guaranteed loan through the Small Business Administration (SBA)
Document 2: Small Business Joint Venturing Act of 2015
amends the Small Business Act to revise requirements with respect to solicitation of offers for bundled contracts
Document 3: study on the participation of small business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals
evaluating whether federal agencies have met the government-wide goals established under the Small Business Act
Document 4: Surety Bond Improvement Act of 2007 - Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958
authorize the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to guarantee any surety against loss
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Act to include Campbell County, Virginia, as a qualified nonmetropolitan county
for purposes of the HUBZone programs under such Act
Document 6: Communicating Lender Activity Reports from the Small Business Administration Act
direct the Small Business Administration (SBA) to make publicly available a user-friendly database of information relating to lenders
Document 7: Small Business Disaster Loan Reporting Act of 2007
Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to report monthly to the congressional small business committees
Document 8: Veterans Entrepreneurial Transition Act of 2015
amends the Small Business Act to direct the Small Business Administration to carry out a pilot program
Document 9: Amends the Small Business Act to exclude from the definition of 'Indian tribe' any Alaska Native Corporation
makes members of socially and economically disadvantaged ANCs or ANVs eligible for such loans
Document 10: Small Business Early-Stage Investment Act of 2009 - Amends the Small Business Investment Act of 1958
to establish and carry out a program to provide equity investment financing to support early-stage small businesses

topic.name: Mortgage Refinancing and Foreclosure Mitigation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at addressing mortgage refinancing opportunities and foreclosure prevention for homeowners, particularly in the context of government-sponsored enterprises.

Document 1: establish the Family Foreclosure Rescue Corporation to acquire home mortgages in default
instructs the Secretary of the Treasury
Document 2: requires the Director to increase the mortgage portfolio limitations
refinancing subprime mortgages at risk of foreclosure
Document 3: increase in the limit on the maximum original mortgage principal obligation
mortgages originated during FY2012 and FY2013
Document 4: providing for the refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages
prohibition on borrower fees
Document 5: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to guarantee or insure loans
increase from $500 million to $1.5 billion the amount of loans
Document 6: authorize Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to purchase affordable workforce housing mortgages
mortgagor income and employment criteria
Document 7: require disclosure of financial information in writing to a consumer
interest rate in effect during the introductory period
Document 8: make grants to states for providing foreclosure-mitigation counseling
homeowners of owner-occupied single family homes with mortgage loans in default
Document 9: revise requirements for public housing agency inspections
assistance to low-income working families
Document 10: terminate the conservatorship of either Fannie Mae or Freddie Mac
require the Director to establish minimum levels of capital

topic.name: Land Exchange and Conveyance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various acts related to land exchanges and conveyances involving federal and state lands for public, conservation, or economic purposes.

Document 1: Requires a land exchange between the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) and the Sabine River Authority of Texas involving the Sabine National Forest.
Requires the costs of the land exchange to be shared equally between the Authority and the Secretary.
Document 2: This bill directs the Department of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to South Dakota in exchange for specified nonfederal lands.
The values of the federal and nonfederal lands to be exchanged shall be equal.
Document 4: Reinstates provisions of the Defense Base Closure and Realignment Act of 1990 which allow military departments to transfer surplus real and personal property.
without consideration to local redevelopment authorities when used for economic development purposes.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey to such Indians all interest of the United States in approximately 40.852 acres of specified federal land.
Requires the Shingle Springs Band of Miwok Indians to pay to the Secretary an amount equal to the market value of the conveyed federal land.
Document 9: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal land in Grand, San Juan, and Uintah Counties, Utah, to the state of Utah in exchange for specified non-federal land.
Sets forth provisions regarding land valuation, appraisal, and equalization.
